Who I Am

About Me

I'm a 2nd year Electronics and Communication Engineering student with a strong passion for building impactful technology. From Android applications to autonomous robots, I love bridging the gap between hardware and software.

My projects span AI-powered systems, real-time fraud detection, and IoT-driven automation — always focused on solving real problems with clean, efficient code.

I'm currently seeking opportunities in Android development and AI/ML engineering where I can grow and contribute meaningfully.

Designed & built by Akash R V · 2025

♿ Smart Wheelchair
An assistive technology designed to help individuals with mobility challenges move independently. Controlled through voice commands and Bluetooth-based mobile control, reducing physical effort and increasing safety. Includes an obstacle detection system to avoid collisions.
Key Components Used
  • Arduino Uno – Main microcontroller that controls all operations
  • Motor Driver (L298N / Driver Shield) – Controls the DC motors
  • DC Gear Motors – Drives the wheels of the wheelchair
  • Bluetooth Module (HC-05 / HC-06) – Enables wireless control via mobile app
  • Ultrasonic Sensor – Detects obstacles in front of the wheelchair
  • Battery (3.7V / 12V) – Provides power to the system
  • Wheels & Chassis – Mechanical support for movement
Software & Programming Languages
Arduino C/C++KotlinJavaAndroid AppBluetooth Protocol
How It Works
The user sends commands via the Android app using voice or button controls. These are transmitted through Bluetooth to the Arduino, which controls the motor driver to move the wheelchair. The ultrasonic sensor continuously checks for obstacles and prevents collisions by stopping or alerting the user.
🤖 Smart Sentinel Robot
An AI-assisted autonomous security and sanitation robot providing real-time monitoring, intrusion detection, and interactive alerts. Moves autonomously, detects human presence via sensors, and triggers audio alerts. Includes a cleaning module for sanitization in homes, offices, and industrial environments.
Key Components Used
  • Arduino Uno – Main controller for processing and control
  • Motor Driver (L298N / Driver Shield) – Controls motor movement
  • DC Gear Motors – Provides mobility to the robot
  • PIR Motion Sensor – Detects human movement in the environment
  • DFPlayer Mini + Speaker – Plays alert sounds, warnings, or voice prompts
  • Bluetooth / Wi-Fi Module (ESP32 / HC-05) – Wireless communication with mobile app
  • Vacuum / Cleaning Motor – Used for cleaning and sanitization tasks
  • Battery Pack – Powers the system
Software & Programming Languages
Arduino C/C++KotlinJavaAndroid AppIoT ProtocolsSerial Communication
How It Works
The robot uses a PIR motion sensor to detect human movement. When motion is detected, the DFPlayer Mini triggers an alert or voice message through the speaker. The robot can be manually controlled through a mobile app and stream live video using the camera module — suitable for security monitoring and smart automation with cleaning support.
🛡️ SentinelPay
A real-time UPI fraud detection and transaction monitoring system designed to identify suspicious financial activity and prevent fraudulent transactions. Evaluates live digital transactions using intelligent risk-scoring logic based on user behavior, transaction patterns, and risk rules.
Key Features
  • Real-time transaction monitoring
  • Risk scoring engine – classifies transactions as Approved, Flagged, or Blocked
  • Profile-based fraud detection rules
  • Behavioural anomaly detection (unusual amount, location, frequency)
  • Secure transaction history with tamper-resistant hashing
  • Alerts and notifications for suspicious activities
  • Interactive dashboard to visualize transaction status and risk levels
Technologies & Programming Languages
KotlinMVVM ArchitectureRoom DatabaseCoroutines & FlowCustom Fraud EngineSHA-256 Hashing
How It Works
When a transaction is initiated, SentinelPay evaluates it using a custom fraud engine that analyzes user profiles, transaction amount, frequency, and behavioral patterns. Each transaction is assigned a risk score and classified as Approved, Flagged, or Blocked. The app displays results in a live dashboard, and suspicious transactions trigger alerts for further verification.
